title: Bank of Zambia (Withdrawal and Exchange of Currency) Regulations, 2025

sections.heading: (1) These Regulations may be cited as the Bank of Zambia Title and
sections.text:
(Withdrawal and Exchange of Currency) Regulations, 2025. commence-
ment
(2) These Regulations shall come into operation on 31st
March, 2025.

sections.heading: Inthese Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.text:
“appointed date” means 31st March, 2025;
“Bank” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Act;
“bank” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Banking Act No. 7 of
and Financial Services Act, 2017: 2017
“existing banknotes” means the existing legal tender in
circulation prior to the appointed date, which shail remain
in circulation until the expiry date;
“expiry date” means 31st March, 2026;
“National Savings and Credit Bank of Zambia” means the
National savings and Credit Bank of Zambia established
under the National Savings and Credit Act; Cap.423
“proliferation financing” has the meaning assigned to the words
in the Anti-Terrorism and Non-Proliferation Act, 2018; Act No. 6 of
2018
“terrorism financing” has the meaning assigned to the words
in the Anti-Terrorism and Non-Proliferation Act, 2018; and Act No. 6 of
2018
“withdrawal” refers to the action of removing or ceasing to
circulate the existing banknotes referred to under these
Regulations.
Copies of this Statutory Instrument can be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O.Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka Price K8.00 each
98 Statutory Instruments 25th March, 2025
Withdrawal 3. (1) The Bank shall commence the withdrawal from
of existing circulation of the following existing banknotes from the appointed
banknotes
date:
(a) K20;
(b) K10;
(c) K5; and
(d) K2.
(2) The Bank shall commence the withdrawal from circulation
of the following existing banknotes from 1st September, 2025:
(a) K100; and
(b) K50.
Exchange of 4. (1) The existing banknotes that are withdrawn under
existing regulation 3 shall be exchanged within the following periods:
banknotes
(a) the existing banknotes referred to under regulation 3(1)
shail be presented for exchange from the appointed date
until the expiry date; and
(b) the existing banknotes referred to under regulation 3(2)
shall be presented for exchange from 1st September,
2025 until the expiry date.
(2) The existing banknotes shall, during the pericds referred
to under subregulation (1), be presented for exchange at the
following locations:
(a) _ the head office in Lusaka and the regional office in Ndola
for the Bank;
(6) acommercial bank within the Republic; or
(c) a branch of the National Savings and Credit Bank of
Zambia.
(3) On the expiry date—
(a) existing banknotes shail not be legal tender within the
Republic; and
(b) existing banknotes shall not be accepted for exchange
at the locations referred to under subregulation (2).
(4) The Bank, a bank or any other business entity shall not
accept the existing banknotes as legal tender after the expiry date.

sections.heading: The exchange of the existing banknotes shall be done free
sections.text:
Charges for
exchange of charge.
25th March, 2025 Statutory Instruments 99

sections.heading: The Bank, banks and the National Savings and Credit Bank Risk
sections.text:
of Zambia engaged in the exchange of the existing banknotes shall management,
money |
establish appropriate risk management systems and take adequate Jaundering,
etc.
measures to address money laundering, terrorism financing,
proliferation financing or any other serious offence.

sections.heading: The Bank may, in conjunction with any other supervisory Inspections
sections.text:
authority, carry out inspections of business entities to ensure
compliance with the Act and these Regulations.

sections.heading: (1) A person shall not exchange the existing banknotes in General
sections.text:
contravention of these Regulations. offence and
penalty
(2) A person who contravenes subregulation (1) or any
provision of these Regulations commits an offence and is liable, on
conviction, to a fine not exceeding five hundred thousand penalty
units or imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years, or to
both.
Dr S. MuSOKOTWANE,
LUSAKA Minister of finance and
21st March, 2025 National Planning
